step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the current conclusion. While passive diffusion indeed plays a key role in facilitating the entry of many small hydrophobic compounds and gases into cells, it should be noted that facilitated diffusion also significantly contributes to the transport of larger polar molecules, sugars, some ions, and even some drugs which may not cross lipid bilayers easily on their own. This additional detail supports the accuracy of choosing option "A" as the correct answer since both forms of simple molecular transport are important depending on the nature of the transported substance.

Answer choice: A.
